However, I can suggest that you may be referring to "Echinacea purpurea", which is a plant species commonly known for its medicinal properties. Here's a paragraph about Echinacea purpurea:

Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant is valued for its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to support the body's natural defenses and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea include alkylamides, glycoproteins, and caffeic acid derivatives, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinacea purpurea include teas, tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, which are often used to boost the immune system and alleviate respiratory tract infections.
